key_insight: A locking-free mixed finite element formulation solves elasticity eigenproblems using only the pseudostress tensor, avoiding any symmetry enforcement and recovering displacement by postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: This provides a robust route for accurate solid mechanics eigenanalysis in 2D and 3D, especially when standard displacement-based FEM suffers from locking.
method: Formulate the elasticity eigenproblem purely in terms of pseudostress.
Discretize with tensor-valued H(bdiv) mixed finite elements based on standard element families.
Prove convergence, a priori error bounds, and a posteriori reliability/efficiency under non-compact operator theory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence and error estimates, plus numerical tests across different geometries and physical configurations to validate the method.
watch_item: Because displacement is recovered through postprocessing, implementation must carefully preserve stability and accuracy in the tensor-based mixed discretization.
paper_title: A locking free mixed FEM based on a pure pseudostress based formulation for the elasticity eigenproblem
